description_long: This object is a classic corded telephone, featuring a rectangular base with a sloped front panel and a separate handset resting on top. The handset is connected to the base by a coiled cord, which is visibly attached at the left side of the base. The front panel of the base is populated with a grid of raised buttons, likely for dialing, and several additional smaller buttons or indicator lights near the top and right side. The base also includes a recessed cradle area designed to securely hold the handset when not in use. The overall construction appears to be primarily plastic, with the coiled cord likely made of flexible rubber or plastic insulation. The design is utilitarian and robust, suitable for use in offices, reception areas, or other urban environments.
description: A rectangular corded telephone with a coiled cord, raised keypad, and a separate handset resting on a cradle. The base features multiple buttons and indicator areas, all in a uniform light color.
description_view_0: Side view showing the handset resting on the cradle and the coiled cord extending from the left side.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the full keypad layout and the width of the handset across the top.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the handset and cord less visible, showing the flat surface of the base.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the back of the handset and the coiled cord trailing away from the base.
